goals.short_name: B.C.'s tourism industry grows and diversifies

main_services.name: Grow and market a tourism business
Fund tourism infrastructure and community development

goals.short_name: Arts, culture and heritage thrive in every B.C. community

main_services.name: Get funding for arts, culture and creative industries
Visit and explore B.C.'s cultural institutions

goals.short_name: British Columbians participate safely in amateur sport

main_services.name: Get funding and support for amateur sport
Compete or host events in combat sports safely

goals.short_name: B.C. hosts world-class events and celebrates its diversity

main_services.name: Host sport, arts and culture events

acts.summary: Continues the British Columbia Arts Council to provide support for arts and culture, allocate grant funding appropriated by the Legislature, and report annually on the status of arts and culture in B.C.

sections.title: Purposes and powers of the council
sections.description: The council supports arts and culture in B.C. through public education, research, advocacy and awarding grants from money appropriated by the Legislature.

services.name: Apply for an arts grant
services.ms_name: Get funding for arts, culture and creative industries

acts.summary: Protects Provincial heritage sites and objects, archaeological sites, burial places and heritage wrecks in British Columbia, and establishes the permit system for heritage inspections, investigations and alterations.

sections.title: Permits and heritage protection
sections.description: Sets out the Provincial heritage register, protections for heritage sites and objects, and the permit system administered by the Archaeology Branch for heritage inspections, investigations and alterations.

services.name: Get a permit to alter, study or excavate a heritage site

acts.summary: Sets out B.C.'s multiculturalism policy, requires every ministry and government corporation to report annually on initiatives promoting that policy, and authorizes the minister to make multiculturalism and anti-racism grants.

sections.title: Application for grants / Minister may provide grants
sections.description: A not-for-profit organization may apply to the minister for a grant for a program, and the minister may make grants that foster or promote the multiculturalism policies set out in the Act.

services.name: Apply for a multiculturalism and anti-racism grant

sections.title: Additional reports
sections.description: Every ministry and government corporation must submit an annual report on initiatives to promote the Act's multiculturalism policies, and the minister must report annually to the Legislative Assembly.

services.name: Multiculturalism and anti-racism policy, research and annual reporting

acts.summary: Establishes the Athletic Commissioner to regulate professional boxing, mixed martial arts and other prescribed combat sports through licensing of participants and event permits for promoters.

sections.title: Licences
sections.description: A person must hold a valid licence from the commissioner to promote, conduct or hold a professional contest or exhibition, or to participate as a contestant, judge, matchmaker, referee, second or timekeeper.

services.name: Get a licence to promote or compete in professional combat sports
services.ms_name: Compete or host events in combat sports safely

sections.title: Event permits
sections.description: A person must hold a valid event permit from the commissioner to promote, conduct or hold a professional contest or exhibition, subject to security, fee and safety requirements.

services.name: Get a permit to host a combat sport event
services.ms_name: Compete or host events in combat sports safely
